skill.name: code-review-ai-ai-review
skill.description: Guides AI-assisted code review workflows that combine static analysis, security checks, architecture review, performance review, and structured pull request feedback.
skill.summary: Run structured AI-assisted code reviews with security, performance, architecture, testing, and maintainability checks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported extensive command, secret, filesystem, and network indicators, but review found these are in documentation and example automation snippets. No malicious intent or prompt injection attempt was found, but the skill should publish with warnings because users may execute scanners, CI commands, and API-backed review workflows.

medium_findings.title: External Tool Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill includes runnable examples for scanners and shell pipelines. This is legitimate for code review, but users should approve commands before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible inside examples for TruffleHog, SonarQube, CodeQL, Semgrep, and Python subprocess calls. They support the stated workflow, but execution can affect local repositories or CI environments.

medium_findings.title: Example Workflow Uses API Tokens
medium_findings.description: The example CI and Python orchestrator read OpenAI, GitHub, and Anthropic credentials from environment variables. This is expected, but requires careful secret handling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable access is explicit and tied to normal API authentication. I found no evidence that the skill exfiltrates or logs the credential values.

low_findings.title: Static Heuristic Combination Is Mostly Documentation
low_findings.description: The dangerous combination finding is a false positive for malicious intent. The indicators appear in fenced examples, checklists, and review templates.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious patterns are shown as instructional examples for a code review skill. I found no hidden loader, obfuscation, unauthorized endpoint, or instruction to bypass security review.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Windows SAM Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and Windows SAM findings match incidental text in examples and prose. No cryptographic implementation or SAM database access is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain metadata, prompt configuration, and SQL injection review text. I found no file access to Windows SAM and no implemented weak cryptographic algorithm.

dangerous_patterns.title: Subprocess Scanner Orchestration Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The complete example invokes SonarQube and Semgrep through Python subprocess calls. Treat this as operator-approved automation, not autonomous execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are explicit and use fixed scanner commands. They are examples, but they can run external tools if copied into a repository.

dangerous_patterns.title: CI Secret And Review Comment Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The GitHub Actions example uses secrets and posts review comments through GitHub APIs. This is normal CI behavior, but it expands trust boundaries.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow clearly references secrets and GitHub review APIs. The context is legitimate automation, with no evidence of sending secrets to an unrelated service.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Read Of Review Output
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow reads review-comments.json before posting comments. This is expected local CI state, but generated files should be trusted carefully.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem read is confined to a named local review output file. Risk depends on whether that file can be modified by untrusted code.

content.user_title: Review Code With AI-Assisted Quality Gates
content.value_statement: Manual pull request review can miss security, performance, and architecture risks. This skill guides AI-assisted review using static analysis, structured comments, and CI quality gates.
actual_capabilities: Triages pull requests by size, file type, risk, and review depth.
Combines CodeQL, SonarQube, Semgrep, dependency scanning, and secret scanning guidance.
Generates structured review prompts for security, performance, architecture, and maintainability checks.
Defines severity levels and comment formats for actionable pull request feedback.
Provides CI examples for scanner execution, AI review, comment posting, and quality gates.
Covers architecture, microservices, scalability, testing, and OWASP review concerns.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a packaged executable review service.
Scanner results depend on installed tools, repository configuration, and language support.
AI review output requires human validation before blocking or approving changes.
Examples that use tokens, APIs, or shell commands need secure local configuration.

use_cases.title: Security-Focused Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Review authentication, authorization, injection, secrets, dependencies, and OWASP risks before merging sensitive changes.

use_cases.title: CI Review Automation Design
use_cases.description: Plan a pipeline that runs static analysis, AI review, review comments, and quality gates for pull requests.

use_cases.title: Architecture And Maintainability Review
use_cases.description: Check service boundaries, dependency direction, complexity, testability, and compatibility for larger code changes.

prompt_templates.title: Start A Basic Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request for bugs, security concerns, missing tests, and maintainability issues. Prioritize clear findings with file and line references.

prompt_templates.title: Review Security-Sensitive Changes
prompt_templates.scenario: Authentication, authorization, payment, or data access changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these changes as security-sensitive. Focus on access control, input validation, secrets, logging, dependency risk, and exploitability.

prompt_templates.title: Design A CI Review Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding automated review to a repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an AI-assisted code review workflow for this repository. Include static analysis tools, secret scanning, comment rules, and quality gates.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Review System Reliability
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an automated code review pipeline.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this code review automation design for false positives, missed risks, token exposure, prompt injection, tool permissions, and merge-blocking policy.

output_examples.input: A pull request changes login, session handling, and password reset behavior.
output: High severity access control issue with file and line reference.
Medium severity session expiration concern with remediation steps.
Missing tests for password reset abuse cases and rate limits.

output_examples.input: A team wants automated review comments on every pull request.
output: Recommended scanner set for security, quality, dependencies, and secrets.
Quality gate policy for critical findings and large pull requests.
Comment format that separates blockers from advisory feedback.

output_examples.input: A microservice change updates API contracts and database ownership.
output: Architecture risks around service boundaries and compatibility.
Backward compatibility checks for clients and versioning.
Testing recommendations for contract, integration, and rollback paths.

best_practices: Run static analysis before AI review so the model can focus on semantic issues.
Require human approval for security-sensitive findings and merge-blocking decisions.
Keep API keys in CI secrets and restrict review automation permissions.
anti_patterns: Do not let AI review approve security-sensitive changes without human verification.
Do not run scanner commands against untrusted repositories without permission controls.
Do not paste raw secrets, private keys, or production data into review prompts.

faq.question: Does this skill run code review tools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflows and examples that an agent or user can adapt with explicit tool approval.

faq.question: Which tools does it reference?
faq.answer: It references CodeQL, SonarQube, Semgrep, Snyk, Dependabot, GitGuardian, TruffleHog, Qodo, Claude, and OpenAI models.

faq.question: Can it replace human reviewers?
faq.answer: No. It is best used to find issues early and prepare focused review comments for humans.

faq.question: Is it suitable for security reviews?
faq.answer: Yes, with human validation. It covers OWASP risks, secrets, dependencies, authentication, authorization, and threat modeling prompts.

faq.question: What repository access does it require?
faq.answer: Access depends on the implementation. The examples may need source code, pull request diffs, scanner output, and CI secrets.

faq.question: What should teams configure before using it?
faq.answer: Teams should configure scanner tools, secret handling, review permissions, model access, and merge-blocking thresholds.
